title: prunus pendula var. koshiensis
intro: Prunus pendula var. koshiensis is a deciduous shrub or small tree known for its weeping habit and clusters of white, fragrant flowers, native to China and Korea. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Chinese medicine, where it is used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Prunus pendula var. koshiensis include its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate colds, flu, and other infections. Prunus pendula var. koshiensis can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is also used in folk medicine to aid digestion and relieve symptoms of bronchitis and asthma.
